items.title: 'There's one kid in every classroom': Grief program teaches families to heal

children.content: One in 20 Australians under the age of 18 has lost a parent, but families say grief services for children are limited. A national program teaching children how to navigate grief is trying to change that.

gallery.altText: A young boy being carried on the back of his mother.

thumbnail.altText: A young boy being carried on the back of his mother.

items.title: ABC wins 11 categories at Tasmanian Media Awards

image.altText: A group of people in suits and dresses holding awards and smiling for a photo.

children.content: The ABC has won 11 of the 20 categories at this year's Media Entertainment & Arts Alliance (MEAA) Tasmanian Media Awards, while WIN News reporter Brent Costelloe took home the prestigious journalist of the year award.

items.title: 'Completely unrealistic' cuts trouble health groups, as treasurer searches for $700 million in savings

children.content: Health groups fear the impact of hundreds of millions of dollars in cuts expected to be found in the Health Department as part of Treasurer Eric Abetz's state budget operational efficiencies program.

image.altText: A female doctor carrying a stethoscope.

items.title: When the bushfire came, this couple turned on their roof sprinkler and left

children.content: Preparing for an ember attack could extend your home's bushfire survivability rate to above 90 per cent or even closer to 100 per cent, a fire scientist says.

image.altText: Aerial perspective of a coastal home spinkler system on roof surrounded by burned trees under a blue sky.

items.title: 'Bushwalkers have died attempting this track': Instagram post glosses over hike's dangers

children.content: It looks beautiful in the Instagram post with blue skies and picturesque tarns carved by glaciers. But read further and the multi-day Tasmanian hike is considered one of Australia's most challenging bushwalks. In fact, it can be deadly.

gallery.altText: Procyon Peak, Western Arthur Range Dan Broun

thumbnail.altText: Procyon Peak, Western Arthur Range Dan Broun

items.title: Rex Airlines announces service cuts in Victoria and Tasmania

children.content: The embattled carrier has announced it is ceasing some services and reducing others.

image.altText: a plane flies in the sky

items.title: Giant iceberg with shipping containers spotted off Antarctica

children.content: Details of how the shipping containers ended up on a giant iceberg have been revealed in one of the more unusual reports tabled at the annual meeting of Antarctic Treaty nations.

image.altText: A large rectangular iceberg with seven dark dots on it, seen from a satellite photo.

items.title: 'An absolutely pleasant surprise': Why single mums are moving in together

children.content: As rental and living costs surge, and as single mums remain one of the most vulnerable groups in the country, some are moving in together and finding many benefits.

image.altText: Hayly Grimmond and Anja Homburg and their children smile while siting on a bed.

items.title: 'I've had enough': Why this loyal Labor voter is switching to One Nation

children.content: The next opportunity to test support for One Nation in Western Sydney will be the state election on March 13. Already, disillusioned Labor voter Silvana Rota is ready to "give them a go".

articles.title: 'Midwits' and media bust-ups herald One Nation's arrival in SA parliament

children.content: One Nation broke its parliamentary silence to talk about culture wars and political barneys. But not everything went as expected.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit and tie look on at a woman wearing dark top, the man is also seen in a camera viewfinder

articles.title: One Nation set to become federal opposition, poll forecasts

children.content: One Nation would win dozens of seats if a federal election was held today, according to a major opinion poll.

image.altText: A man in an Akubara wraps his arms around a woman with a microphone

gallery.altText: A woman wearing glasses and a purple t shirt

thumbnail.altText: A woman wearing glasses and a purple t shirt

avatar.alt: ABC reporter Nick Dole looking straight at the camera with a grey background

items.title: Maldives cave dive deaths leave us with many theories and one big question

image.altText: A deep-sea diver descends into a cave system, trailing a guide line.

children.content: The question of why experienced divers would descend to depths of more than 50 metres on a single tank of air — let alone enter a cave while doing so — baffles members of the relatively close-knit deep-sea diving community.

items.title: Five economists share opinions on Labor's budget and Coalition's reply

children.content: Five leading economists weigh in on the budget and reply debate, embracing many of the ideas raised while also arguing that "bigger tax reform is needed."

image.altText: A man in a suit stands at a podium in parliament.

items.title: This private island paradise has a bargain price tag, and problems galore

children.content: Makri Island, not far from mainland Greece, boasts almost 100 hectares of uninhabited Mediterranean beauty, but buyers be warned, this slice of paradise comes with a catch.

image.altText: An island, seen from above

items.title: Fifty years ago, a killer returned the knife to the kitchen and slipped away

children.content: Teenage Dawn Hillier was a "ray of sunshine". Almost 50 years later, years of investigation, a coronial inquiry, and a big reward have been unable to solve her brutal murder.

gallery.altText: A graphic showing murder victim Dawn Hillier

thumbnail.altText: A graphic showing murder victim Dawn Hillier

avatar.alt: A headshot image of ABC reporter Ciara Jones.

items.title: Trump revives centuries-old doctrine that shaped US-Cuba relations

children.content: The Monroe Doctrine was once a core tenet of US foreign policy. US President Donald Trump has brought it back with a new interpretation.

image.altText: Trump gives a thumbs up.

items.title: Auckland makes A-League history with grand-final triumph over Sydney

children.content: Auckland has become the first side from New Zealand to win an A-League Men championship after downing competition heavyweight Sydney 1-0 in a cagey season decider.

image.altText: A soccer player in blue and black screams in celebration with floodlight behind him

items.title: Jamie-Lee Price thinks fake tan could solve a lot of netball's problems

children.content: Frustrated at netball's stagnation, this is what Jamie-Lee Price wants to see happen to keep her sport moving with the times.

gallery.altText: Netballer Jamie-Lee Price holds a netball and stares at the camera.
Jamie-Lee Price of the Mavericks passes the ball.
Netball Jamie-Lee Price sits alone in a court.

items.title: Meet the women turning bicycle tyres and timber offcuts into furniture

children.content: These artists are carving out a niche in a traditionally male space, using recycled materials and offcuts to create beautiful furniture, and even making music to accompany the work.

image.altText: A black and white photo of a woman at a work bench constructing wooden furniture.

items.title: Put away the dictionary. It's spelling bee time!

children.content: Know your way around a homonym? Nah, me neither but come in and have a crack at our Sunday quiz anyway.

image.altText: Guy Montgomery beams as he stands in front of the Guy Mont set in front of a podium bearing a cup, purse and bucket.

items.title: Bones found by Thai villager belonged to 27m 'Nagatitan'

children.content: It started out as a bone sticking out of the mud next to a lake in Thailand — and now scientists say it's the biggest dinosaur ever found in South-East Asia.

gallery.altText: An enormous brown bone on its end stands slightly taller than a Thai man next to it wearing all black and sneakers.
Diagram of a long-necked dinosaur skeleton in black, with a human for scale, smaller than its legs. A few bones are yellow.
Wide shot of two men in a store room with shelves behind them, one holding a bone and the other flicking through a book.

thumbnail.altText: An enormous brown bone on its end stands slightly taller than a Thai man next to it wearing all black and sneakers.
Diagram of a long-necked dinosaur skeleton in black, with a human for scale, smaller than its legs. A few bones are yellow.
Wide shot of two men in a store room with shelves behind them, one holding a bone and the other flicking through a book.

items.title: Death toll reaches 82 after Chinese coal mine blast

children.content: A gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China's Shanxi province sits at 82, state media reports. Two people remain missing.

image.altText: A yellow school bus on a road

items.title: Mystery billboard that left officials 'scratching heads' solved

children.content: Amid months of debate about the location for the 2032 Olympic rowing events, a billboard came to life. Who put it there and why was a mystery until now.

image.altText: A billboard with cartoon crocodiles rowing next to the words 'push off penrith'

items.title: 'There's more in the tank:' Pendlebury celebrates games record despite Eagles scare

children.content: Collingwood's No.10 enjoys a 10-point win in his record-breaking match as the Magpies hold firm to down the Eagles at the MCG.

image.altText: An AFL player in black and white stripes is carried off the field on the shoulders of two others. Camera flash to the left

items.title: 'Stay with us': Dragons booed off the field at home ahead of fan day

children.content: St George Illawarra is 0-11 to start the 2026 season after losing to the Warriors in Kogarah, while the Sea Eagles continue their strong form with a hard-fought victory over Gold Coast.

image.altText: Dragons players look tired during an NRL game.

items.title: Iran war lays bare overlooked holes in Australia's sovereign capability

children.content: The Iran war has not only highlighted the vulnerability of global supply chains — it has also exposed the overlooked parts of Australia's manufacturing sector needed in a crisis.

gallery.altText: An assembly line at a meatworks, workers in head to toe PPE are cutting lamb and completing other tasks

thumbnail.altText: An assembly line at a meatworks, workers in head to toe PPE are cutting lamb and completing other tasks

items.title: Man allegedly armed with gun shot dead by police north of Brisbane

children.content: Queensland Police said the man approached officers and made threats towards police whilst armed with a firearm, and as a result was shot.

image.altText: Police tape in front of a narrow road with police cars behind it.

items.title: From farm gate to stage door, theatre is a release from life on the land

children.content: Putting their livestock, tractors and drought feeding aside for a night, these farmers are taking to the stage.

image.altText: Man with akubra looks at camera, pink sky and field in background.

items.title: Socceroos duo's Premier League dream dashed as Hull beat Middlesbrough

children.content: Middlesbrough's two Socceroos, Riley McGree and Sammy Silvera, have been left shattered after all the drama surrounding the Championship play-offs ended with a deflating, last-gasp 1-0 defeat to Premier League-bound Hull City at Wembley.

image.altText: Two players battle for the soccer ball which is at chest height

items.title: Vingegaard takes control of Giro as Aussie Hindley makes move

children.content: Giro d'Italia favourite Jonas Vingegaard has taken the leader's pink jersey with his third stage win of the race while Australia's former champ Jai Hindley made a significant move towards the podium in Pila.

image.altText: Cyclist crossing the finish line, exerting effort shown in his facial expression

items.title: Mercedes duo clash as Piastri pulls off stunning overtake on Hamilton

children.content: George Russell says he did nothing wrong in a hard-fought Canadian Grand Prix sprint battle with Mercedes teammate Kimi Antonelli after the Italian questioned the Briton's defending.

items.title: PM defends negative gearing, capital gains tax changes at Labor conference

children.content: The prime minister became emotional at one point as he spoke at a Labor conference in Melbourne to defend the budget changes.

image.altText: A close-up image of Anthony Albanese looking serious in front of a microphone

items.title: Children's entertainer Danny Go!'s son dies of cancer aged 14

children.content: The popular children's YouTuber revealed the news after documenting the illness since it was diagnosed late last year. Tributes have flowed from Children's entertainers, including the Wiggles.

image.altText: The YouTuber shared the news of his son's diagnosis in December, last year.
